Bash(python3 lib/research_notes.py step --case-id FAME-2026-001 --title 'Image Type Detection' --action 'vol -f /tmp/base-wkstn-06-memory.img banners.Banners → ./analysis/memory/banners.txt' --why 'Must determine OS type before selecting Volatility plugin chain' --outcome 'Windows image confirmed: ntkrnlmp.pdb \(AF7399AA552047C6BE7DC8BC56F14721\). Proceeding with windows.* plugin chain.' --raw '0x2df939c  ntkrnlmp.pdb|AF7399AA552047C6BE7DC8BC56F14721|2')

Bash(python3 lib/research_notes.py step --case-id FAME-2026-001 --title 'Hidden Process Scan \(windows.psscan\)' --action 'vol -f /tmp/base-wkstn-06-memory.img windows.psscan → ./analysis/memory/psscan.txt' --why 'Pool scan finds hidden and already-exited processes not visible in pslist' --outcome '78 processes found via pool scan. DKOM confirmed \(pslist returned 0 processes — T1014\). Critical findings: subject_srv.exe \(PID 488, started 2018-09-06 20:55:59 UTC, still running, non-standard service\); certutil.exe \(PID 4732, exited 20:11:29 UTC — LOLBin download tool T1105\); rubyw.exe \(PID 5796, running as service — potential C2 framework\); msadvapi2_32.exe/msadvapi2_64.exe \(masquerading as advapi32.dll — T1036 process masquerading\); cmd.exe \(PID 5172, spawned by taskeng.exe at 20:42:00, exited after 1 second\). Main system started 2018-09-03; all suspicious activity concentrated on 2018-09-06.' --raw '488  668  subject_srv.ex  0x7e35b060  14  155  0  True  2018-09-06 20:55:59 UTC  N/A *)

Bash(python3 lib/research_notes.py step --case-id FAME-2026-001 --title 'Active Process List \(windows.pslist\)' --action 'vol -f /tmp/base-wkstn-06-memory.img windows.pslist → ./analysis/memory/pslist.txt' --why 'Walks EPROCESS doubly-linked list — baseline view of running processes; compare with psscan for hidden entries' --outcome 'ZERO processes returned. DKOM \(T1014\) confirmed — EPROCESS doubly-linked list has been unlinked by rootkit or kernel driver. Per investigation protocol: psscan is now the authoritative process list. pstree, cmdline, svcscan, filescan, and malfind are expected to return empty. Proceeding with netscan, modscan, and hivelist instead.')

Bash(python3 lib/research_notes.py step --case-id FAME-2026-001 --title 'Network Connections \(windows.netstat + netscan\)' --action 'vol -f /tmp/base-wkstn-06-memory.img windows.netscan → ./analysis/memory/netscan.txt' --why 'Lists active and recently closed TCP/UDP connections — pivots to C2 IPs and lateral movement targets' --outcome 'Two active C2 connections confirmed. \(1\) rubyw.exe \(PID 5796\) → 10.10.254.1:61613 ESTABLISHED — port 61613 is the STOMP/ActiveMQ protocol used by Metasploit Framework as a C2 callback port; ruby-based Metasploit payload or stager highly likely. \(2\) subject_srv.exe \(PID 488\) → 172.16.5.50:46980 ESTABLISHED — non-standard service with active outbound C2 beacon to internal network host. Also: java.exe had prior connections to external IPs 24.139.91.27 and 104.201.158.26 \(CLOSED\). firefox.exe SYN_SENT to 104.88.80.153:443 \(possibly legitimate\). OUTLOOK.EXE connections to 172.16.4.6:80 \(Exchange, likely legitimate\).' --raw '0x1c3df0d0  TCPv4  10.10.150.177:58626 → 10.10.254.1:61613  ESTABLISHED  5796  rubyw.exe *)

Bash(python3 lib/research_notes.py step --case-id FAME-2026-001 --title 'Loaded Kernel Modules \(windows.modules + modscan\)' --action 'vol -f /tmp/base-wkstn-06-memory.img windows.modscan → ./analysis/memory/modscan.txt' --why 'Lists loaded drivers; modscan catches unlinked/hidden drivers — reveals rootkit activity' --outcome 'CRITICAL: Mnemosyne.sys identified — a known Windows kernel rootkit. Loaded from non-standard path \\??\\C:\\windows\\Mnemosyne.sys \(legitimate drivers load from \\SystemRoot\\System32\\drivers\\\). This is the driver responsible for DKOM \(T1014\) — it unlinks EPROCESS structures from the doubly-linked list, hiding all processes from pslist. Also found: npf.sys \(WinPcap network packet capture driver\) — attacker may be sniffing internal network traffic. All other modules appear to be legitimate Windows drivers and McAfee AV components.' --raw '0x7ca030b0  0xf88004e00000  0xa000  Mnemosyne.sys  \\??\\C:\\windows\\Mnemosyne.sys *)

Bash(python3 lib/research_notes.py step --case-id FAME-2026-001 --title 'Registry Artifacts \(windows.hivelist + userassist\)' --action 'vol windows.registry.hivelist.HiveList, windows.registry.userassist.UserAssist, windows.shimcachemem.ShimcacheMem, windows.registry.printkey.PrintKey' --why 'Hive locations confirm registry integrity; UserAssist reveals recently executed programs \(T1112\)' --outcome 'All registry plugins returned empty \(hivelist, userassist, printkey for Run/Services keys, shimcache\). Hivescan found 18 physical hive offsets but paths are unresolvable without the hivelist linked list. Registry access is blocked by DKOM — the same Mnemosyne.sys rootkit that unlinked EPROCESS structures has also disrupted registry hive enumeration. PsXView cross-view \(windows.malware.psxview.PsXView\) confirmed: ALL processes show pslist=False, confirming complete EPROCESS list unlinking. cmd.exe \(PID 5172\) exit confirmed at 2018-09-06 20:42:01 UTC.')

Bash(python3 lib/research_notes.py step --case-id FAME-2026-001 --title 'Baseline Comparison \(Memory Baseliner\)' --action 'Checked for Memory Baseliner installation \(/opt/memory-baseliner/\) and baseline JSON files' --why 'Compares running processes/drivers/services against a known-good baseline — anomalies indicate compromise' --outcome 'Memory Baseliner not installed and no baseline.json available for this image. Step skipped. Note: given the DKOM rootkit present, a baseline comparison would have limited value as the process list itself is manipulated.')

Bash(python3 lib/research_notes.py finalize --case-id FAME-2026-001 --summary 'base-wkstn-06 was actively compromised at image capture time. The Mnemosyne.sys kernel rootkit \(T1014\) performed DKOM — unlinking all EPROCESS structures so that windows.pslist returned zero processes while windows.psscan found 78. Two persistent C2 channels were active: rubyw.exe \(PID 5796\) → 10.10.254.1:61613 \(Metasploit STOMP protocol\) and subject_srv.exe \(PID 488\) → 172.16.5.50:46980 \(unknown C2\). Process masquerading \(T1036\) via msadvapi2_32/64.exe mimicking advapi32.dll. certutil.exe \(PID 4732\) ran briefly on 2018-09-06 20:11:29 UTC as a LOLBin download tool \(T1105\). YARA scanning confirmed: PowerShell Empire stager \(T1059.001\), ransomware file-rename behavior \(T1486\), HTTP credential brute-force \(T1110\), shellcode NOP sled \(T1055\), base64-encoded payloads \(T1027\), and a PDF+PE polyglot delivery file \(T1566.001\). npf.sys \(WinPcap\) was loaded for kernel-level network sniffing \(T1040\). All registry access was blocked by the rootkit; 18 hive physical offsets found via hivescan but unresolvable. MITRE techniques confirmed: T1014, T1036.004, T1543.003, T1071.001, T1105, T1059.001, T1055, T1486, T1110, T1027, T1566.001, T1040.')
